description: Subtle notes of peach rise from the glass and gain full form on the palate where the flavors are boosted by gentle sweetness. The palate is round, fresh and off dry. Drink while aromas are at their peak.
title: Joseph Fritsch 2016 Gewurztraminer (Alsace)

description: Light and steely, this wine offers mild flavors that lean tropical, like pineapple and guava. Those succulently ripe layers meet stone fruit and a medium-sized body, making for an enjoyable, food-friendly experience on the palate.
title: Clif Family 2016 Rte Blanc Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)

description: Most of the estate vines used for this blend date back to the mid-1970s. It emphasizes bright citrus flavors, like lemon and lime, with lemon verbena, cilantro and other fresh herb notes that add detail to the finish. A very fine value, it can also age for a decade or longer.
title: Elk Cove 2016 Estate Riesling (Willamette Valley)

description: In its singular oval bottle, this is the top cuvée of this prestigious estate close to Saint-Tropez. It is a lively and fruity wine with great acidity and richness. It has spice as well as red-currant and raspberry fruitiness. This is definitely a wine for food, with its edge of structured acidity and bone dry aftertaste. Drink from late 2017.
title: Les Maîtres Vignerons de la Presqu'île de Saint-Tropez 2016 Château de Pampelonne Légende Rosé (Côtes de Provence)
winery: Les Maîtres Vignerons de la Presqu'île de Saint-Tropez

description: Quite pale and pink in color, this wine has more flavor and body than the color would suggest. It blends cherry and ripe pear notes on a smooth mouth-filling texture that clings to the palate and extends the finish.
title: Yorba 2016 Shake Ridge Vineyards Rosado (Amador County)

description: A blend of Aragonez, Syrah and Touriga Nacional, this is a crisp wine, full of raspberry fruits and a soft texture. It is attractive with its balance of acidity and fruit, and is very ready to drink.
title: Ciconia 2016 Ciconia Rosé (Alentejano)

description: Medicinal cherry and plum aromas set up a palate with clipped, hard tannins. Forced flavors of oak and carob are more obvious than the wine's plum and cassis notes. Oak dominates the creamy, charred finish.
title: Finca El Origen 2016 Estate Malbec (Mendoza)

description: Earthy berry aromas come with woodspice notes. This is raw and acidic on the palate, and overall it feels prickly and a bit disjointed. Saucy plum flavors are salty, while the punch of red-fruit acidity propels a tangy finish.
title: Los Andicos 2016 Selección Especial Malbec (Mendoza)

description: Six months in wood have changed the naturally crisp character of the Arinto grape for something altogether much richer. It offers spice as well as ripe red apples and peppered pears. Drink now.
title: Casca Wines 2016 Cabo da Roca Reserva Arinto (Bucelas)

description: A stainless-steel wine that's attractively priced and easy to like, this is bright, juicy and stony, highlighted in peach and nutmeg. The crisp acidity provides tension and body.
title: Cline 2016 Estate Grown Pinot Gris (Sonoma Coast)

description: Pale in color, this crisp wine offers a touch of yeastiness that contrasts with the red-currant fruits. It has plenty of bright acidity as well as a tangy, zesty aftertaste.
title: Château Fonfroide 2016 Rosé (Bordeaux Rosé)

description: A blend of simple stone-fruit and citrus aromas exhibits a touch of vegetal green. This feels balanced but highly regular, while briny citrus and persimmon flavors hang around on the finish.
title: Viña Maipo 2016 Vitral Reserva Chardonnay (Chile)

description: This blend of 89% Sauvignon Blanc, 10% Chardonnay and 1% Muscat is fulsome, lush and rounded, with deep layers of pear, fig and vanilla flavors. Acidity provides a zippy end to its lengthy finish.
title: Bennett Lane 2016 Maximus White Feasting Wine White (Napa Valley)

description: Made with rich Encruzado, full of apricot and creamed pear flavors, this is a full-bodied wine. Its smooth texture is matched by a lift of acidity and an orange-zest flavor. The wine is going to be ready to drink from 2018.
title: Quinta do Sobral 2016 Santar Encruzado (Dão)

description: Crisp lemon, green apple and riper stone-fruit aromas are hidden behind a slightly reductive and rubbery touch on the nose of this new brand from the Coquelicot team. Grassy flavors show on the sip alongside Anjou pear.
title: Rose & Son 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Santa Barbara County)
